The unfinished state of Angkor Wat after the death of its Hindu patron, King Suryavarman II, left both physical and symbolic space for transformation. Unlike other temples, Angkor Wat was never fully abandoned and continually hosted a mix of Hindu and Buddhist rituals, with Buddhist images gradually replacing or standing alongside Hindu ones.
